question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open a large number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions (each calling listen()) with no server-side subscriber cap, causing the shared Schedulers.boundedElastic() thread pool to be fully exhausted, making the mirror node unresponsive to all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with limit=0 (no limit) and a very old startTime, causing RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) to fire DB queries indefinitely at every interval, saturating the database connection pool and causing a non-network DoS for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to the earliest possible consensus timestamp, causing findByFilter() to execute a full table scan on the topic_message table on every poll interval, exhausting DB CPU and I/O and causing a DoS for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of subscriptions each with limit=0 and no endTime, causing maxPageSize=5000 rows to be loaded into JVM heap on every poll for every subscription, leading to JVM OutOfMemoryError and a complete service crash?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessageRepositoryCustomImpl.findByFilter() calls getResultList() (loading all results into memory) rather than a streaming cursor, by subscribing with a high-volume topic and no limit, causing repeated heap allocations of up to maxPageSize=5000 TopicMessage objects per poll cycle per subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) repeat count combined with a minimal interval (500ms default) to generate approximately 2 DB queries per second per subscription indefinitely, and by opening N concurrent subscriptions generate 2N DB queries/second, causing database CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an integer underflow in the expression (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) when count exceeds limit due to a race condition, producing a negative pageSize that is passed to Math.min(), resulting in unexpected query behavior or an exception that crashes the reactive pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user set a very large limit value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) causing the expression (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) to overflow to a large positive integer, bypassing the intended page size cap and causing the DB to return far more rows than maxPageSize per poll?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with endTime set far in the future (e.g., year 2100 in nanoseconds), keeping each subscription alive indefinitely and accumulating PollingContext objects in memory, causing a slow memory leak that eventually triggers a DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Schedulers.boundedElastic() instance (shared across all PollingTopicListener instances) by opening many concurrent subscriptions, each consuming a scheduler thread via delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) and withScheduler(scheduler), starving legitimate subscribers of scheduler threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic with extremely high message throughput (e.g., a topic receiving thousands of messages per second), causing each poll to return maxPageSize=5000 messages, and with many concurrent subscribers cause the JVM heap to be overwhelmed by concurrent result list allocations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a back-pressure mechanism in Flux.defer(() -> poll(context)) combined with RepeatSpec to cause unbounded message accumulation in the reactive pipeline buffer, exhausting JVM heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter with startTime=0 (epoch) and no endTime, causing the JPA criteria query in findByFilter() to scan the entire topic_message table from the beginning of time on every poll, causing sustained high DB I/O and a DoS for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions without ever consuming (reading) the emitted messages, causing the reactive pipeline to buffer up to maxBufferSize=16384 messages per subscription in memory, and by opening many such subscriptions exhaust JVM heap?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByFilter() calls entityManager.createNativeQuery(TOPIC_MESSAGES_BY_ID_QUERY_HINT).executeUpdate() on every poll when limit != 1, causing excessive DDL-like session state mutations on the DB connection pool under high concurrency, degrading DB performance for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with limit=1 (which skips the TOPIC_MESSAGES_BY_ID_QUERY_HINT hint) and then immediately re-subscribe in a tight loop after the subscription completes, causing a high rate of new subscription setups and DB queries that overwhelm the server?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: PollingContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicLong count in PollingContext to cause count.get() to overflow Long.MAX_VALUE in a very long-running subscription on a high-throughput topic, causing the limit calculation (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) to produce a negative value and break the polling logic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the jitter(0.1) in RepeatSpec to cause polling bursts where multiple subscriptions fire DB queries simultaneously (due to jitter alignment), creating periodic DB query spikes that cause transient DoS for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a non-existent topicId, causing findByFilter() to execute a DB query that returns empty results on every poll interval indefinitely (since there is no termination condition for empty results), wasting DB resources and scheduler threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open many subscriptions to non-existent topics, each polling indefinitely and returning empty results, causing the boundedElastic scheduler to be saturated with idle-but-active polling tasks that never terminate?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.fromStream() wrapping of a JPA result stream, where the underlying JDBC connection is held open until the stream is fully consumed, causing DB connection pool exhaustion when many concurrent subscriptions are active?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum concurrent subscription limit per client IP or per topicId to open an unbounded number of subscriptions, causing linear growth in DB query rate and memory usage proportional to the number of subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to exactly 1 and repeatedly re-subscribe after each message is received, causing a high rate of new PollingContext object allocations and subscription setups that generate excessive GC pressure and degrade JVM performance?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that each listen() call creates a new independent PollingContext and Flux pipeline with no shared resource throttling, allowing N concurrent subscriptions to generate exactly N independent DB query streams with no rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the toBuilder() call on the filter inside poll() to create a new filter object on every poll cycle, causing excessive object allocation and GC pressure under high subscription counts?
